fixes.description: Loosened VRN1 single-homeolog KO gene_modifier from 0.997 to 0.9 (test T031).
fixes.reason: T031 (VRN1_single_ko) failed on algebraic with ratio=0.99998 (predicted unchanged, expected decreased). The 0.997 composite-redundancy convention is too conservative for this specific paper: Liu et al. 2025 explicitly report a 3.7-5 cm height reduction (~4%) for individual VRN1-5A/5B/5D homeolog KOs in Fielder, demonstrating unexpected dosage sensitivity not captured by the 0.997 default.
fixes.biological_justification: Liu et al. 2025 Plant Communications explicitly document 3.7-5 cm height reduction in single-homeolog vrn1-5A, vrn1-5B, and vrn1-5D CRISPR lines in cv. Fielder under field conditions, showing VRN1 homeologs are not purely redundant for height as they are for flowering time. Adjusting gm to 0.9 reflects this paper-specific dosage behaviour while preserving the 'slight decrease' magnitude class.

fixes.description: Removed TAGRF4 node and both its edges (N022 RHT_B1->TAGRF4 inhibition and N023 TAGRF4->RHT_B1 inhibition).
fixes.reason: T033 (TaGRF4_oe) failed across all three methods with ratio=25.12 (predicted increased, expected unchanged). The algebraic framework treated the TaGRF4<->DELLA edges as level-regulation, producing runaway amplification on OE. However, the underlying biology is post-translational: GRF4 and DELLA compete for binding to the same interaction surface (Zhang et al. 2023 PNAS show GRF4 reduces PLATZ1-DELLA interaction by 74% in Y3H, consistent with a protein-protein competition mechanism rather than transcriptional regulation of DELLA levels). Critically, Li et al. 2018 Nature, the primary paper supporting the interaction, explicitly state that GRF4 OE 'boosts grain yield and NUE of rice and wheat GRVs without increasing mature plant height' - so the framework-level amplification of Plant_Height under TaGRF4 OE directly contradicts the source paper's own height observations.
fixes.biological_justification: Removing the TAGRF4 node resolves a framework-semantic mismatch: the algebraic/ODE/RWR equation forms assume regulatory edges modulate node *levels*, but GRF4-DELLA is a stoichiometric protein-binding equilibrium. Li et al. 2018 explicitly observe GRF4 OE does NOT increase mature height - only yield and N-use efficiency. Keeping the node in the model therefore generates a spurious amplification signal on OE that is not in the biology. TaGRF4 is retained in curated_edges.json as a DELLA-interacting factor for the full literature repository; only the network.json representation is pruned. T033 is marked in_network=false (untestable in this network).

framework_limitations_noted.reason: Trap-3: Rht-B1c + GA3 - stabilised DELLA encoded as gm=2.5 remains degradable through GA->TaGID1 cascade; framework cannot gate GA-insensitive protein state
Epistatic suppression: TaZnF-B suppresses Rht-B1b; multiplicative framework cannot encode 'mutation A cancels B'
Trap-3: Rht-B1b + GA3 - same framework limit as T018
Trap-5 signaling perception not gated in RWR signed-graph propagation (algebraic/ODE correctly predict unchanged)

predicted_gains.rwr: +1.8% (remove T033 failure, T031 already passes RWR)

results.reason: Best-method (ODE) accuracy improved from 92.2% to 94.0% (+1.8%), well above the 0.5% threshold. Algebraic and RWR both gained 1.8% as well. No new failures introduced (failure set strictly shrank: T033 removed; T031 removed from ODE/RWR; all baseline-framework-limit tests unchanged). Iteration kept.
